description: Daniel Flury was appointed Operations Director for HMCTS in March 2024, leading nearly 15,000 people working across courts, tribunals and national services. Daniel joined the civil service in 1999 and has held a number of operational and policy positions within HMCTS, the Legal Aid Agency and the Ministry of Justice. As well as a HMCTS Cluster Manager, Transformation Director and Delivery Director, Daniel has also been the Programme Director of three Major Government programmes (including the HMCTS Crime Programme) and is a graduate of the Major Projects Leadership Academy. He has also been the Service Owner for Tribunals and for Crime and the Access to Justice Policy Director in MoJ.

details.body:
Daniel Flury was appointed Operations Director for HMCTS in March 2024, leading nearly 15,000 people working across courts, tribunals and national services.

Daniel joined the civil service in 1999 and has held a number of operational and policy positions within HMCTS, the Legal Aid Agency and the Ministry of Justice.

As well as a HMCTS Cluster Manager, Transformation Director and Delivery Director, Daniel has also been the Programme Director of three Major Government programmes (including the HMCTS Crime Programme) and is a graduate of the Major Projects Leadership Academy. He has also been the Service Owner for Tribunals and for Crime and the Access to Justice Policy Director in MoJ.

Daniel Flury was appointed Operations Director for HMCTS in March 2024, leading nearly 15,000 people working across courts, tribunals and national services.

Daniel joined the civil service in 1999 and has held a number of operational and policy positions within HMCTS, the Legal Aid Agency and the Ministry of Justice.

As well as a HMCTS Cluster Manager, Transformation Director and Delivery Director, Daniel has also been the Programme Director of three Major Government programmes (including the HMCTS Crime Programme) and is a graduate of the Major Projects Leadership Academy. He has also been the Service Owner for Tribunals and for Crime and the Access to Justice Policy Director in MoJ.
Responsible for operational delivery of all HMCTS court, tribunal and national services and HMCTS’ implementation of criminal court reforms from the Independent Review of the Criminal Courts.

Daniel Flury was appointed Operations Director for HMCTS in March 2024, leading nearly 15,000 people working across courts, tribunals and national services.

Daniel joined the civil service in 1999 and has held a number of operational and policy positions within HMCTS, the Legal Aid Agency and the Ministry of Justice.

As well as a HMCTS Cluster Manager, Transformation Director and Delivery Director, Daniel has also been the Programme Director of three Major Government programmes (including the HMCTS Crime Programme) and is a graduate of the Major Projects Leadership Academy. He has also been the Service Owner for Tribunals and for Crime and the Access to Justice Policy Director in MoJ.

The Head of Criminal Casework leads the teams that process all applications and bills for criminal legal aid and central funds. This also includes the collection and enforcement of contributions made under the Crown Court means testing scheme.

The post-holder is a member of the Executive Leadership Team, chairs the LAA’s Operational Committee and has membership of the LAA board.

role_appointments.title: Daniel Flury - Head of Criminal Casework
